summary.short_rationale: Akimov has narrow exported matrix signal, but the clean gap is strategically useful: enterprise AI adoption roadmaps and practical process transformation. Coding-agent workflow is a duplicate/supporting area.
summary.strongest_argument_for: Source spot checks support grounded adoption frameworks: AI literacy before mega-projects, employee-built assistants, no-code/low-code/custom escalation, data/process readiness, and staged business value models.
summary.strongest_argument_against: Only two matrix_export cells are present; one is an exact duplicate in an already dense coding-agent area.

accepted_basis_cells.interpretation: clean matrix gap for enterprise AI adoption roadmaps, process readiness, data quality, no-code/low-code/custom escalation, and staged AI value realization

duplicate_or_supporting_cells.interpretation: already covered as strong multi-source coding-agent workflow coverage

non_exported_supporting_cells.interpretation: normal-weight OCR + LLM document parsing pattern; not a primary admission basis

good_fit: chat access vs no-code vs low-code vs custom AI development decisions
business process redesign and AI literacy
non-developer AI coding with engineering-practice guardrails
poor_fit: broad model-release news when stable strategy is needed
local LLM deployment or MCP internals

risk_notes: One of two exported cells is an exact duplicate in a dense coding-agent area.
Tool pricing, limits, and vendor recommendations age quickly.
Adoption-roadmap value should be probed before strong production routing.
recommended_manifest_caveat: accepted for enterprise AI adoption roadmaps and practical AI-product/process transformation; agentic coding workflow overlaps existing experts, avoid broad duplicate routing

probe_shape: 2 enterprise AI adoption / failed rollout queries
1 chat access vs no-code vs low-code vs custom development query
1 data/process readiness query against Air, Ostrikov, and Polyakov
1 agentic-coding workflow query where Akimov should be supporting
